justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified — 99.9% Onfido dependency makes 99.95% team SLA 'mathematically impossible', with specific numbers (0.05% gap, ~26 min/year). (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended: async KYC with 'pending_verification' state, circuit breaker with queue fallback, and implicit SLA revision via escalation to Coach K. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'primary blocker for production readiness' with cascading failure analysis. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling identified — notes that health check failures during Onfido outage can cause 'total service death' from orchestrator restarts. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to Coach K for the SLA business decision.

observations.text: Excellent SLA math analysis with concrete numbers: composite availability capped at 99.9%, 0.05% gap = ~26 minutes/year of promised-but-undeliverable uptime
Circuit breaker gap thoroughly analyzed with cascading failure scenario: thread/connection pool exhaustion leading to full service death, not just KYC failures
Health endpoint coupling correctly identified — Onfido downtime causes health check failures triggering unnecessary orchestrator restarts
Multiple concrete mitigation paths: pending_verification state, circuit breaker with async queue, adaptive timeouts, retry with backoff for 429s
Identified additional failure modes beyond the rubric: thundering herd after recovery, rate limiting (429), malformed responses
Escalations correctly routed with clear business framing — SLA gap is 'not if but when'
Operational readiness section includes actionable next steps: on-call runbook, queue infrastructure for async fallback, migration for pending state
